revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:富知六所浅間神社]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Fuji Rokusho shaden — 2018.jpg | caption = Birds eye view of the shrine with a view of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} in the background. | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | name = Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine | image =
{{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} and Shrine Buildings {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|10|1.66|N|138|40|32.93|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, [[:en:Fuji City|Fuji City]], Sengen Honmachi 5-1 | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|1.66|N|138|40|32.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Main deity = | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (small), [[Beppyo Shrine]], Former [[Gokoku Shrines|Gōsha]] | founded = (Traditional) 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} | main hall = | alternate name = Mikkaichi Sengen Shrine | Saisho = | festival = {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|qid=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} | ceremony = }} '''{{ill|Fuji Rokusho Sengen Shrine|ja|富知六所淺間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Rokusho Sengen Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 富知六所浅間神社}}''' is a [[Beppyo Shrine]]. It was founded in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} but was moved due to an eruption in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It worships [[Ōyamatsumi]] Rokusho shrine (six place) is a very common Soja shrine name{{cite book |last1=Hardacre |first1=Helen |title=Shinto: A History |date=2016 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0190621711}}.{{Rp|page=601}} ==Overview== '''Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine''' (富知六所浅間神社, Fujirokusho Sengen Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Fuji City|Fuji City]], {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is one of the [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] and a designated [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Commonly referred to as '''Mikkaichi Sengen Shrine''' (三日市浅間神社), it is part of the [[Sengen shrine|Sengen shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}}. It is the primary shrine among the "Five Sengen Shrines of Fuji" (富士下方五社) and serves as the head shrine. == Deities == ; Main deity * '''[[Ōyamatsumi]]''' - deity of mountains ; Secondary Deities * [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] - Daughter of Ōyamatsumi * {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}} - Descendant of Ōyamatsumi * [[Awanome no Mikoto]] - Wife of [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} - Descendant of Ōyamatsumi * [[Kuraokami]] - Water deity Usually, "Sengen Shrine" refers to a shrine primarily enshrining Konohanasakuya-hime, but this shrine primarily enshrines her father, Ōyamatsumi. During the period of syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism, Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai was the principal deity (honji-butsu). == History == According to tradition, the shrine was established on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} in the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. Due to volcanic eruptions, it was relocated to its current location in the 4th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} (785). Emperor [[:en:Suinin|Suinin]] sent Takenukawawake-no-Mikoto to pay respects and offer imperial donations. A market called "Mikkaichi Sengen" (Three Day Market) developed around the shrine. According to records written in 1767, it was said that Emperor [[:en:Heizei|Heizei]] established the Five Shrines, including this one, in the 1st year of [[:en:Daido|Daido]] (806). Originally called "Rokusho-no-Miya" or "Rokusho Sengen-no-Miya," it was renamed Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine in the 5th year of Meiji (1872) when it was designated as a Gōsha. During the medieval period, it was commonly referred to as "Rokusho-no-Miya". In a map from the 2nd year of {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} (1862), it is labeled as "Fuji Rokusho Sengen-no-Miya, approximately 8 chō from {{ill|Yoshiwara-juku|en|Yoshiwara-juku|qid=Q3069833|lt=Yoshiwara-juku|1=Yoshiwara-juku}}." The shrine is one of the Five Sengen Shrines of Fuji and was directly managed by the Tōsen'in temple (now abolished). The shrine was considered the head shrine of the Five Sengen Shrines and was described as the "main shrine" in the "Fuji Mountain Great Origin" written in the 13th year of {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|qid=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} (1560). However, the influence and jurisdiction over the shrine shifted over time. After the [[Takeda clan]] took control of the area, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|qid=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} granted the shrine to Kunō-dera temple in the 1st year of {{ill|Genki|en|Genki|qid=Q21501757|lt=Genki|1=Genki}} (1570), a reward for prayers for military success. When the Takeda clan fell and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} was transferred to the Kanto region, the area came under the influence of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. During this period, Tōsen'in was granted 190 koku in the 18th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1590). This 190 koku included lands of the Five Sengen Shrines, indicating the shrine returned under the influence of Tōsen'in. However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} (1600), a seal document shows that 190 koku were granted to Chishōbō, an inke of Kunō-dera, indicating the shrine fell under Kunō-dera's influence again. The shrine is considered one of the possible locations of the "Fuji Shrine" listed in the [[Engishiki]]'s list of shrines. == Major festivals == * [[:en:New Year festival|New Year festival]] (January 1) * ceremony for Burning Old Amulets and Dondoyaki festival (January 14) * Great Camphor Tree festival (April 29) : The festival honors the shrine's sacred tree, a camphor tree over 1200 years old and designated as a natural monument of Shizuoka Prefecture. The festival expresses gratitude for nature. * {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|qid=Q132241|lt=Festival|1=Festival}} (May 3) : The period around Golden Week sees numerous visitors, with related festivals held on May 1 and May 2. * {{ill|Yoshiwara Gion festival|ja|吉原祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshiwara Gion festival|qq=}} (2nd Saturday and Sunday of June) : This festival involves shrines under the management of Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine. The festival draws over 200,000 visitors over two days. * Summer festival (Mid-August during Obon period) : The festival includes a memorial service for the war dead and prayers for world peace. Local vendors participate, and performances are held in front of the shrine. * Onomachi festival (1st Saturday of October, or the next day if it rains) : This autumn festival is organized by the shrine's neighboring parishioners and features performances and stalls. == Grounds == * Shrine Buildings : The current main and worship halls were completed in October 2015. * Sacred Tree : To the east of the shrine buildings stands a sacred camphor tree, estimated to be over 1200 years old. It is 11.85 meters in circumference and 13.4 meters in height and is designated as a natural monument of Shizuoka Prefecture. == Access == ; location * 5-1 Sengen Honmachi, [[:en:Fuji City|Fuji City]], {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} ; Transportation : By rail - About a 20-minute walk from [[Yoshiwara-Honchō Station]] on the {{ill|Gakunan Railway Line|en|Gakunan Railway Line|qid=Q5517715|lt=Gakunan Railway Line|1=Gakunan Railway Line}} : By car - About a 10-minute drive from [[Fuji IC]] on the {{ill|Tōmei Expressway|en|Tōmei Expressway|qid=Q1135694|lt=Tōmei Expressway|1=Tōmei Expressway}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author = Fuji City Museum|year = 2015|title = Rokusho Family Comprehensive Survey Report|publisher = Fuji City Board of Education|isbn = }} == External Links == *[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4404103 Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine] (Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association) *[https://twitter.com/fuji6sho_lab?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine Official Twitter] *[https://www.instagram.com/fuji6sho_lab/?hl=ja Fujirokusho Sengen Shrine Official Instagram] {{Shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Asama Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] [[File:Fuji Rokusho shaden — 2018.jpg|250px]] [[File:Fuji Rokusho Sengen Jinja Honden.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Haiden]] {{everybodywiki link|Fuji Rokusho Sengen Shrine}} [[simple:Fuji Rokusho Sengen Shrine]] [[ja:富知六所浅間神社]] {{translated page|ja|富知六所浅間神社|version=105634579|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11457276}} [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]]
